title: Recover an AGV That Lost a Tote Without Faulting
summary: Restore an AGV and tote to service after an AGV loses a tote without faulting by stopping RMS, manually moving the AGV to the Hospital, removing and re-adding the AGV at the Hospital station PC, power cycling the AGV, positioning it over the QR code in front of the Inbound Hospital Station, and removing and re-adding the missing tote so the AGV can return the tote to the sorter.
when_to_use: Use when an AGV has lost a tote without faulting and must be restored to service through the Hospital workflow described in section 5.8.2.6.
not_for: Do not use when the AGV is faulted; the packet references separate faulted-AGV recovery content.
Do not use when the missing tote cannot be removed or added back using the documented tote procedures.
Do not use when the AGV cannot be physically moved to the Hospital or cannot be re-added through the Hospital station PC.

responsibilities: Use the AGV brake release and manually move the AGV to the Hospital.
Remove and re-add the AGV at the Hospital station PC/Hospital HMI.
Power cycle the AGV and verify the AGV lights turn green.
Position the AGV over the QR code in front of the Inbound Hospital Station.
Locate the missing tote and complete tote removal and add-back using the documented procedures.

safety_notes: This procedure is not marked support-safe in the candidate.
The procedure includes E-stop use, brake release use, manual AGV movement, and AGV power cycling.
Only maintenance or a supervisor should remove an AGV from the system, per the Add/Remove AGV source reference attached to the Hospital HMI screen artifact.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the AGV brake release
Access to the Hospital station PC
Access to the Inbound Hospital Station QR code location
Access to documented Add/Remove AGV procedure
Access to documented Remove Tote procedure
Access to documented Add Tote procedure

access_required: Hospital station PC / Hospital HMI access
Physical access to the affected AGV and Hospital area
system_preconditions: The affected condition is an AGV that lost a tote without faulting.
The missing tote can be located for Hospital handling and re-entry.

visual_references.description: Recovery-related AGV/tote screen artifact associated in the packet with section 5.8.2.6 on page 91.
AGV API Controls screen showing Remove AGV and Recover AGV controls.
System screen artifact relevant to RMS stop/shut-down state context.
Recovery screen artifact showing related AGV recovery context and Hospital/add-agv workflow references.
Hospital HMI screen for tote scanning and REMOVE TOTE workflow.
Hospital Station Operation artifact for tote handling at the Hospital station.
Hospital HMI Add/Remove AGV screen used to remove and insert AGVs into the system.
Add Tote-related artifact showing tote barcode scan and Confirm & Execute workflow.

steps.title: E-stop the RMS
steps.purpose: Place the system into a stopped state before performing manual AGV recovery actions.
steps.instruction: E-stop the RMS before beginning recovery of the AGV that lost a tote without faulting.
steps.expected_result: RMS is in an E-stopped or shut-down state suitable for recovery work.
steps.healthy_condition: The system is stopped and recovery actions can proceed.
steps.failure_condition: RMS cannot be E-stopped or the system state does not allow safe recovery progression.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System shut-down mode visual context for stopped system state.
Recovery-related screen context showing E-stop use in AGV/tote recovery.

stop_or_escalate_if: The system does not enter a stopped state needed for recovery.

steps.title: Release the AGV brake
steps.purpose: Allow the AGV to be manually moved to the Hospital.
steps.instruction: Press the brake release on the AGV that lost a tote.
steps.expected_result: The AGV brake is released so the AGV can be rolled manually.
steps.healthy_condition: The brake release can be used and the AGV is movable.
steps.failure_condition: The brake release cannot be used or the AGV remains immovable.

actions.label: Press brake release
actions.exact_action: Press the brake release on the AGV that lost a tote.

stop_or_escalate_if: The brake release cannot be used.
The AGV cannot be prepared for manual movement.

steps.title: Move the AGV to the Hospital
steps.purpose: Bring the affected AGV to the Hospital area for re-registration and tote recovery handling.
steps.instruction: Push the AGV over to the Hospital.
steps.expected_result: The AGV is physically positioned at the Hospital.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV reaches the Hospital area and is available for Hospital station PC actions.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be moved to the Hospital.

actions.label: Push AGV to Hospital
actions.exact_action: Push the AGV over to the Hospital.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital station area context for tote handling and AGV arrival.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV cannot be moved to the Hospital.
The Hospital area cannot be accessed for recovery.

steps.title: Remove and re-add the AGV at the Hospital station PC
steps.purpose: Re-register the AGV in the system from the Hospital interface.
steps.instruction: At the Hospital station PC, remove the AGV and then add the AGV back into the system.
steps.expected_result: The AGV is removed and then reinserted into the system through the Hospital station interface.
steps.healthy_condition: The Hospital station PC/Hospital HMI accepts the AGV removal and add-back actions.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be removed or cannot be added back into the system.

actions.label: Remove AGV
actions.exact_action: At the Hospital station PC, remove the AGV.

actions.label: Add AGV back
actions.exact_action: Then, add the AGV back into the system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: AGV ID entry and the Hospital HMI controls used to remove an AGV and INSERT INTO SYSTEM.
Related AGV Remove AGV and Recover AGV controls context.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Hospital station PC/Hospital HMI does not allow AGV removal.
The AGV cannot be added back into the system.
Required AGV management controls are unavailable.

steps.title: Power cycle the AGV
steps.purpose: Reset the AGV after re-registration so it can return to a healthy state.
steps.instruction: Turn the AGV off, then turn it on again.
steps.expected_result: The AGV powers back on and proceeds toward a normal status indication.
steps.healthy_condition: After the power cycle, the AGV lights turn green.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV lights do not turn green after the power cycle.
stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV does not power back on.
The AGV lights do not turn green after the power cycle.

steps.title: Position the AGV over the Inbound Hospital Station QR code
steps.purpose: Place the AGV at the specified location for continued system handling after recovery.
steps.instruction: When the AGV lights turn green, roll it over the QR code in front of the Inbound Hospital Station.
steps.expected_result: The AGV is positioned over the QR code in front of the Inbound Hospital Station.
steps.healthy_condition: AGV lights are green and the AGV is correctly positioned over the QR code.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV does not reach green-light status or cannot be positioned over the QR code.

actions.label: Roll AGV to QR code
actions.exact_action: When the AGV lights turn green, roll it over the QR code in front of the Inbound Hospital Station.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV lights do not turn green.
The AGV cannot be positioned over the QR code in front of the Inbound Hospital Station.

steps.title: Locate the missing tote and remove it from the system
steps.purpose: Recover the missing tote record through the Hospital tote-removal workflow before re-adding it.
steps.instruction: Locate the missing tote and bring it to the Hospital to remove it from the system.
steps.expected_result: The missing tote is at the Hospital and has been removed from the system using the documented tote-removal process.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote is found, brought to the Hospital, and successfully removed from the system.
steps.failure_condition: The missing tote cannot be located or cannot be removed using the documented procedure.

actions.label: Bring tote to Hospital
actions.exact_action: Locate the missing tote and bring it to the Hospital.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital HMI tote scan fields and REMOVE TOTE button.
Hospital station tote handling flow, including scanning tote barcode and extracting tote from the system.

stop_or_escalate_if: The missing tote cannot be located.
The tote cannot be removed from the system using the documented tote procedure.

steps.title: Add the tote back into the system
steps.purpose: Return the tote to active service so the AGV can transport it back to the sorter.
steps.instruction: Add the tote back into the system. The AGV should then take the tote back to the sorter.
steps.expected_result: The tote is added back into the system and the AGV takes the tote back to the sorter.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote add-back is accepted and the AGV returns the tote to the sorter.
steps.failure_condition: The tote cannot be added back into the system or the AGV does not take the tote back to the sorter.

actions.label: Add tote back
actions.exact_action: Add the tote back into the system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Add Tote screen workflow: scan tote barcode, verify Tote ID, and press Confirm & Execute.
Hospital station tote placement context if the tote is going back into the system.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote cannot be added back using the documented Add Tote procedure.
The AGV does not take the tote back to the sorter after tote add-back.

success_criteria: The AGV has been removed and added back into the system at the Hospital station PC.
The AGV lights turn green after the power cycle.
The AGV is rolled over the QR code in front of the Inbound Hospital Station.
The missing tote is located, removed from the system, and added back into the system.
The AGV takes the tote back to the sorter.
healthy_conditions: RMS can be E-stopped for recovery.
The AGV brake release works and the AGV can be moved to the Hospital.
The Hospital station PC/Hospital HMI accepts AGV removal and add-back.
The AGV lights turn green after power cycling.
The tote can be removed and re-added using the documented tote procedures.
After tote add-back, the AGV takes the tote back to the sorter.
failure_conditions: AGV cannot be moved to the Hospital.
AGV cannot be removed and added back at the Hospital station PC.
AGV lights do not turn green after the power cycle.
Missing tote cannot be removed and re-added using the documented tote procedures.
After tote add-back, the AGV does not take the tote back to the sorter.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the brake release cannot be used or the AGV cannot be moved to the Hospital.
Escalate if the AGV cannot be removed and added back at the Hospital station PC.
Escalate if the AGV lights do not turn green after the power cycle.
Escalate if the missing tote cannot be located or cannot be removed and re-added using the documented tote procedures.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital HMI Add/Remove AGV controls for AGV removal and insertion into system.
AGV API controls including Remove AGV and Recover AGV context.
Hospital HMI tote scan fields and REMOVE TOTE control.
Hospital station tote handling and placement context.
Add Tote workflow including barcode scan and Confirm & Execute.

missing_details: The packet does not provide the exact RMS resume step for this specific procedure, so no resume action was added.
The packet does not provide the exact Hospital station PC field-by-field sequence for AGV removal/add-back in section 5.8.2.6 beyond remove then add back.
The packet does not provide a visual artifact for the QR code in front of the Inbound Hospital Station.
The packet does not provide an estimated completion time.
The packet does not explicitly state whether production stop or LOTO is required for this procedure.
